input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: A blank screen transitions to a close-up of a white rectangular baking dish holding white fish fillets covered in creamy sauce. The dish is garnished with yellow lemon zest, green dill sprigs, and black pepper. A text overlay reads 'LEMON BUTTER Baked Cod' in green and script fonts alongside a 'Bake it With Love' logo in the lower right corner. The scene remains entirely static for the duration with no camera movement or alteration to the food presentation.

[16-18] The scene cuts to a metal saucepan on a black stovetop where a chunk of yellow butter melts. A blue spatula stirs the bubbling liquid. White text on the stove surface reads 'REMAINS HOT AFTER USE' near the pan handle. The butter dissolves into a clear yellow liquid as the spatula moves.

[18-20] The metal saucepan remains on the black stovetop as yellow butter chunks continue melting within bubbling liquid. The blue spatula previously stirring is no longer visible, leaving the butter to melt undisturbed. White text reading 'REMAINS HOT AFTER USE' is clearly visible on the stove surface near the handle.

[20-22] A blue spatula re-enters the metal saucepan, stirring melting yellow butter chunks within the bubbling liquid. The utensil moves solid pieces around the pan as they dissolve on the black stovetop. White text reading 'REMAINS HOT AFTER USE' remains visible near the handle.

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
